blocks.content: Oblivious, Rian fanned the flames in Olina's anxious heart.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Father, instead of this, why don't we go chop some wood now in celebration of Shirone's return!

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Haha! Should we? Alright, let's set the woods ablaze, just us men!

blocks.speaker: Olina
blocks.content: Honey! You've been drinking, and now chopping wood? Just go inside and sleep it off.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Mom, it's okay. I'll be back soon.
Shirone readily agreed.
Given the situation, letting off some steam seemed a good opportunity.
On the other hand, Olina couldn't help but worry.

blocks.speaker: Olina
blocks.content: Even though Vincent was strong with alcohol, climbing a mountain in an inebriated state was perilous even for experienced woodsmen.

blocks.speaker: Olina
blocks.content: Are you sure you'll be okay? What if you get hurt?

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: I'll be there, so it'll be fine. We'll be back before lunch.
Olina's words would have been enough to dissuade Shirone in the past, but now things were different.
He had faced fearsome Ulks and protected children, so he was confident nothing worrisome would happen.
Olina found her son's newfound confidence unfamiliar.
He seemed brighter and more self-assured in his speech and actions.
Was this the power of education? He had always been a good son but often hard to read.
Now, he seemed like a completely different person, seemingly loving himself a bit more.
With Olina's reluctant permission, the three men set off to the logging area, exchanging stories they hadn't shared in a while.
Rian, eager to show off his progress, quickened his pace.
During his two-day stay at Vincent's, Rian had heard about Shirone's childhood tales, especially impressed by the Thunder Strike.
Having struggled against Sword Smash in a duel with Shirone at the main house, Rian's interest was piqued.
Upon reaching the logging area, Rian shouted.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Alright! Let's chop some wood like true men! How about it, Shirone? Wanna bet? The loser grants a wish.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Sure, why not.
Rian lifted the axe and nodded at Vincent, who responded by giving a thumbs-up signal.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Then I'll start.
Rian swung the axe with all his might.
A thud echoed as the tree trunk shook violently.
An almost lethal destructive power.
It was clear how rigorous his training had been for the past six months.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Wow, amazing. Is that Schema?

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Huh? Ahaha! Of course not! Why would I need Schema for chopping wood?
Though Rian's reply was somewhat awkward, Shirone chose to let it pass.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: The real show starts now.
Rian's axe struck the same spot where the bark had burst open.
With a loud crack like thunder, the tree fell in one strike.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Thunder Strike...
Shirone recognized it immediately.
Rian's success with Thunder Strike meant he could now perform Sword Smash.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Hahaha! How about that, Shirone? I'm not the same as before!
Despite his boastful shout, Rian's heart was racing.
There was a slight chance of failure.
He had learned Sword Smash at the swordsmanship academy, but its success rate was much lower against human opponents.
However, after hearing about Thunder Strike from Vincent and practicing all day, he had improved to a point where he succeeded nine out of ten times against trees.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Impressive. Your Thunder Strike has a different impact from mine.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Of course, it's only natural. Anyway, it's a bet. I succeeded in two tries, so you have to bring it down in one!
Vincent burst out laughing heartily.
Haha! Give it your best, Shirone. If it's a tie, we'll continue until one of you wins. We might end up clearing the whole mountain at this rate!
Vincent thought Shirone would be no pushover.
While Rian's strength was remarkable, Shirone had always had a natural talent for technical skills since he was young.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Here, use my axe.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No, it's fine. I'll do it this way.
Shirone approached the tree and extended his hand.
Vincent and Rian looked puzzled. How could he bring down a thick tree without an axe?
Shirone entered his Spirit Zone and used Windcutter, a basic magic, to strike the tree.
A snapping sound came from inside the tree trunk.
And that was that.
As the two onlookers gaped in confusion, Shirone gently pushed the tree, revealing a clean cut as it fell over.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yay! I win!
While Shirone jumped for joy, Vincent and Rian stood with their mouths agape.
How could a tree be cut down without any physical blow? Unless magic was used, it was an impossible feat.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Ah, so that was magic.
Rian inspected the cleanly cut base of the tree.
Even a swordsmanship trainee couldn't have made such a smooth cut.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: What kind of magic is that?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's called Windcutter. It sharpens the air by creating wind, but actually, it's quite a basic magic.
Vincent was lost for words at the transformation in his son.
Of course, he had sent Shirone to a magic academy, so he had expected him to learn some magic, but he had only imagined something akin to a circus trick.
But to cut down a tree in one go? Depending on how it's used, it could be as deadly as a weapon.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Hehe. Unbelievable. Can you teach me that magic? We could chop way more wood with that.
Vincent's innocent remark left Shirone smiling awkwardly.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's basic, but not so easy to learn.
Mastering Windcutter would naturally enable one to chop over a hundred trees a day.
However, no mage ever chopped wood for money.
Even if they cut down a thousand trees, a mage's daily wage was much higher.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone felt good realizing this fact.
When he first entered school, he was overwhelmed by the fear of failure, but now he felt reassured, knowing he had skills to support his family even if he couldn't graduate.
Others might think a mage who had opened the Immortal Function was crazy to consider such things, but for Shirone, his family was that precious.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Anyway, since I won, I'll make a wish. Rian, you carry the wood back home.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Tch! Can't help it. I was going to carry it anyway.
Rian chopped the fallen tree into pieces and prepared to carry it back on a yoke.
Despite losing the bet, a smile lingered on his lips as he descended the mountain.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Amazing, Shirone. Of course, what you showed today isn't all you've got, right?
Back home, Olina had prepared a feast of delicacies from land, sea, and air.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Wow, this looks delicious. Thank you for the meal!
While eating, Shirone shared his experiences at school.
He naturally omitted life-threatening situations, but there were still many things to tell.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: ...so the correct answer was actually number 3. Hahaha! Thanks to that, I'll be advancing to Class Four from next semester.
Unexpectedly, Vincent and Olina didn't show much reaction.
Shirone, realizing they might not have grasped the significance, spread his hands and emphasized.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's a really big deal.
Only then did the expressions on their parents' faces brighten.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Ah! That's great, Shirone! Congratulations!

blocks.speaker: Olina
blocks.content: Well done, our son. I was so worried.
Olina dabbed her eyes with the tablecloth, moved to tears.
Rian watched the loving family scene with a pleased smile, understanding where Shirone's calm temperament originated.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: By the way, Rian, why did you really come here?

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Ah, right. I came to ask your parents for permission.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: You can ask anything. If it's something we can help with, we surely will.
Vincent said this, though he was quite worried.
Why would the son of a noble family ask permission from a woodsman?

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Can I go on a trip with Shirone? For about a week.
Shirone turned in surprise.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What, a trip? Why are you talking about something I don't even know? Where are you planning to go?

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Galliant Island. Don't worry. I've already planned it out.
Galliant? Really?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The place that had recently occupied his thoughts was now being mentioned by Rian.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: I was stuck in the library when working. Let's get some fresh air this time. It's close to Creas and will be fun. Galliant Island is one of the most popular resorts in the world.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: And there's the Kergos Ruins too.
This time, Rian was surprised.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Uh? You knew about that?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No, I just knew there were ruins there. Oh, it's that famous, huh? That's unexpected.
Shirone had been scouring maps for the past few days and found out about the Kergos Ruins on Galliant Island.
He had planned to visit them during the vacation anyway.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone glanced at his mother. He had intended to visit at some point during the break, but announcing a trip right after returning home for half a year might disappoint them.

blocks.speaker: Olina
blocks.content: Go ahead. We fully support you.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: But Mom, I was planning to help Father with his work after returning from school...

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: You lad! Did you go to a battlefield or something? It's just a school you're returning from. It's okay to go on a trip during the break. It's not good for a young man to stick too close to his parents.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: So, can I stay for ten days?

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Ten days? That's fine, but it seems a bit long for just sightseeing, doesn't it?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Ah, actually, I have something I want to research there.
Rian looked exasperated.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: You'll be studying there too? You really are something, seriously.

blocks.speaker: Rian
blocks.content: Hehe, but it won't be easy this time. Will he really be able to study there?
Thinking of his secret plan, Rian smiled to himself, eagerly awaiting the next day.
